10. INDEMNITY

10.1. The Consultant will defend and indemnify the Company, its affiliates, representatives from and against any claim or action commenced by a third party alleging that the Services or Deliverables provided infringes copyrights, trademark, patent or other intellectual property and will indemnify Company, its affiliates, representatives from any costs, [including reasonable attorneys' fees], which result from the defense or settlement of and/or damages finally awarded against the Company, its affiliates, representatives in any such claim.

10.2. The Consultant warrant not to sue, or file any file a claim or action against the Company or its assignee for the use of Deliverables alleging that the Services or Deliverables provided infringes copyright, trademark, patent or other intellectual property that belong to the Consultant.
11. MISCELLANEOUS

11.1. In the event it shall be determined under applicable law that a certain provision set forth in this Agreement is invalid or unenforceable, such determination shall not affect the remaining provisions of this Agreement unless the business purpose of this Agreement is substantially frustrated thereby.

11.3. The Parties of this Agreement are not in employment relationships, but independent Consultants and will have no power or authority to assume or create any obligation or responsibility on behalf of each other.
11.4. No amendment and/or modification of this Agreement shall have any effect unless such amendment or modification shall be done in a written document, signed by both Parties.

11.5. This Agreement shall be construed in accordance with the laws of Norway, without reference to its conflict of laws provisions, and the competent courts of Norway, shall have exclusive jurisdiction over any dispute arising from this Agreement.
11.6. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which when executed will be an original, but all of which together will constitute one and the same instrument.

2. The Processor's duties
2.1. In consideration of performance of all of the Consultant's obligations hereunder, the Consultant shall be entitled to monthly fee as stipulated in Annex B to this Agreement.

2.1. The Processor confirms that it will implement appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that processing will meet the requirements of the Personal Data Regulation and ensure the protection of the rights of the data subject, including complying with the requirements in Article 32 of the General Data Protection Regulation.

2.2. The Processor shall only process the personal data under the instructions given by the Controller.
2.3. Each party shall be responsible for payment of their own taxes.

2.3. The Processor shall, considering the nature of the processing, assist the Controller by appropriate technical and organizational measures, insofar as this is possible, for the fulfillment of the Controller's obligation to respond to requests for exercising the data subject's rights laid down in Chapter III of the General Data Protection Regulation, provided that processing will be perform within the assets controlled by the Controller.
2.4. The Consultant is an independent contractor and shall, unless otherwise specifically stated in this Agreement, pay all own expenses.

2.5. Upon prior approval of the Company, Consultant may receive compensation of reasonable business expenses related to provision of Services to the Company including office space or traveling expenses.

2.6. The Processor has a duty of confidentiality regarding the personal data and other information the Processor receives as part of the Data Processing Terms and the processing of personal data, and shall ensure that persons authorized to process the personal data have committed themselves to confidentiality or are under an appropriate statutory obligation of confidentiality.

2.7. The Processor shall not transfer or give access to the personal data or information which the Processor processes or handles on behalf of the Controller to a third party without the explicit instruction from the Controller.

3. TERM AND TERMINATION
3. The Company shall be responsible for payment of all transaction fees.

3. The Services provided under this Agreement shall be provided on a retainer basis and the Consultant will dedicate a significant and the necessary time to provide the Services to a satisfactory standard.
3. Use of subcontractor/sub-processor

3.1. The Processor shall not engage another supplier for the processing of the personal data, [sub-processor], without prior specific or general written authorisation of the Controller, and the sub-processor has confirmed that it undertakes to implement appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that processing will meet the requirements of the Personal Data Regulation and this Terms and ensure the protection of the rights of the data subject.

3.1. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue in full force and effect unless terminated.This Agreement may be terminated by either party’s prior written notice with a termination term of 60 days.
3.2. Any approved sub-processors are included in the Appendix to this Data Processing Terms.

3.2. The Company may terminate this Agreement without any prior notice and without being obliged to pay any sum as would have been payable to the Consultant, if the Consultant committed at any time any act of fraud, embezzlement, disparagement, or misappropriation against the Company.
3.3. Both Parties are entitled to cancel the Agreement with immediate effect in case of material breach of the contract.
3.3. The Controller has given the Processor a general written authorisation for the use of sub-processors for processing personal data under the Data Processing Terms.

3.4. Any sub-processor shall be imposed the same obligations as the Processor set forth in the Data Processing Terms in a written, binding agreement where in particular the sub-processor is providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the processing will meet the requirements of the Personal Data Regulation and this Terms.

3.4. Should this Agreement be terminated, Consultant shall return to the Company any and all equipment, [including software accounts], and confidential information made available to it, if applicable, by the Company in order to provide the Services according to the Company's instructions.

4. Payments shall be made within 10 days of the received invoice.
4. Security of processing and notification of breach
4. USE OF COMPANY’S SYSTEMS
4.1. During the term of this Agreement the Consultant may be provided access to the Company’s information systems.
4.1. The Processor shall comply with the requirements to security given in the Personal Data Regulation.
4.2. In case of personal data breach, the Processor shall without undue delay notify the Controller.

4.3. If not all information above may be given in the first notice, the information shall be provided as soon as possible.

4.4. The Consultant hereby consents to having personal data transferred to third parties, including to third parties outside EU / EEA area, in connection with due-diligence processes, in connection with legal disputes etc.

4.4. The Controller is responsible for notifying the supervisory authorities, such as Datatilsynet in Norway, and the Processor is not to contact or notify the supervisory authorities without the explicit instruction by the Controller.
5. PROFESSIONAL SECRECY

5. 5. Transfer to third countries

5.1. The Consultant is obligated to maintain complete secrecy towards persons not concerned, regarding all information the Consultant has gained about the Company or other companies in the same group, or about any of the Company’s customers, concerning commercial matters or technology, or any issues of an internal nature.
5.2. The Consultant accepts that the Agreement contains extensive obligation to maintain secrecy concerning several matters.
5.3 The obligation to maintain secrecy applies both during the Service Period and after the termination of the Agreement, whether terminated lawfully or not, without limitation of time.

5.4. The Consultant acknowledges that any breach of confidentiality at any time may lead to liability and may constitute grounds for termination of this Agreement and/or render the Consultant liable to legal action.
6. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
6. Term. Instruction to stop the processing.
6.1. Transfer of rights.

6.1. Upon breach of these Data Processing Terms, of instructions given by the Controller or on the Personal Data Regulation, the Controller may instruct the Processor to stop the processing of the personal data with immediate effect.
6.2. All the intellectual property rights, [including exclusive rights], and ownership in and to the Deliverables shall belong to the Consultant.

6.2. Upon termination of Agreement, regardless reason, the Processor, [and its permitted sub-processors], shall delete or return any or all personal data to the Controller, subject to the Controllers prior request and instructions, in a standardized format and medium along with necessary instructions to facilitate the Controller’s further use of such data, and delete all copies of those personal data.
6.3. Should the Consultant create any patentable work or invention, he shall immediately inform the Company.

6.3. The Controller shall receive a written confirmation from the Processor that all personal data has been returned or deleted according to the Controller’s instructions and that the Processor has not kept any copy, print out or any other representation of such data on any medium.

6.4. The Consultant shall not and shall not authorize any third party any claim or action in court of any jurisdiction whatsoever that Company’s use of Deliverables under this Agreement infringing Consultants intellectual property rights, [including copyright, trademark and patent rights]
7. LIABILITY

7.1. For non-fulfilment or improper fulfillment of own liabilities under this Agreement, the responsible Party will compensate another Party caused and documentary approved damages to the fullest extent with the maximum possible compensation in the amount, paid or owed upon the Agreement regardless of the execution of the obligations under this Agreement and/or the elimination of violations.
7.2. Irrespective of the preceding paragraph liability shall be governed by the relevant provisions of the Norwegian Civil Code.
8. NON-SOLICITATION AND NON-COMPETE

8.1. The Consultant will not, for the duration of this Agreement and thereafter for a period of 6 months commencing on the date upon which the Agreement ceases, be concerned or participate in, whether as a consultant, employee in any other capacity, any business which directly compete or will compete with the Company’s software for hosting and management of online, [virtual], events.

8.2. The Consultant agrees that during the term of this Agreement and for 12 months after its termination, he/she shall not, directly or indirectly, induce or attempt to induce any customer, supplier, Company, licensee, or business relation of the Company to cease doing business with Company, or in any way harmfully interfere with the relationship between any customer, supplier, Company, licensee, and the Company.

8.3. The Consultant will not, for the duration of this Agreement and for 12 months thereafter, directly or indirectly on its own account or on behalf of or in conjunction with any person, approach any employees or consultants of the Company with a view to inducing such persons to terminate their employment or consulting relationship with the Company.
8.4. The Consultant acknowledges and agrees that the fee paid under this Agreement is sufficient enough for effectiveness and applicability of this clause.

8.5. The Consultant shall be free to take employment with or to be engaged as a consultant in any other company provided that such engagement or employment does not constitute a breach of this clause of this Agreement, or in any way prevents or detracts from the fulfillment of the Consultant's obligations according to this Agreement.
8.6. In the event of breach of the Consultant's duties regulated in this Clause, the Company may demand that the breach be discontinued and may claim liquidated damages from the Consultant.

9.1. Each Party is independently liable for observing the applicable legal requirements for the lawful processing of personal data in the context of its activities undergone for the purposes of this Agreement.
